content: lastnode  Table of contents Prototype: lastnode(string, separator) Return type: string Description: Returns the part of string after the last separator . This function returns the final node in a chain, given a regular expression to split on. This is mainly useful for finding leaf-names of files, from a fully qualified path name. Arguments: string : string - Input string - in the range: .* separator : string - Link separator, e.g. /,: - in the range: .* Example: code body common control { bundlesequence => { "yes" }; } bundle agent yes { vars : " path1 " string => "/one/two/last1" ; " path2 " string => "one:two:last2" ; " path4 " string => "/one/two/" ; " last1 " string => lastnode ( " $(path1) " , "/" ); " last2 " string => lastnode ( " $(path2) " , ":" ); " last3 " string => lastnode ( " $(path2) " , "/" ); " last4 " string => lastnode ( " $(path4) " , "/" ); reports : "Last / node in / path ' $(path1) ' = ' $(last1) '" ; "Last : node in : path ' $(path2) ' = ' $(last2) '" ; "Last / node in : path ' $(path2) ' = ' $(last3) '" ; "Last / node in /-terminated path ' $(path4) ' = ' $(last4) '" ; } Output: code R: Last / node in / path '/one/two/last1' = 'last1' R: Last : node in : path 'one:two:last2' = 'last2' R: Last / node in : path 'one:two:last2' = 'one:two:last2' R: Last / node in /-terminated path '/one/two/' = '' See also: filestat() , dirname() , splitstring() .
